summary.intent: Fix six Codex review thread findings on PR #732 (ars-ui Tabs component), push the fixes, resolve all threads, and retrigger Codex review while polling until a terminal state.
summary.outcome: All six fixes were implemented (Tailwind indicator regressions, root `group` leakage, Leptos custom renderer same-key refresh, E2E helper WebDriver error masking), tests passed, changes committed as 'Fix Tabs review follow-ups', and a fresh `@codex review` was triggered with 5-minute polling cadence underway.

repo: The project uses a `.agents/skills/waiting-for-codex-review/SKILL.md` skill that defines a polling loop protocol including terminal states (👀 active, 👍 approved, new threads = findings).
Codex review is triggered via `@codex review` PR comment and tracked via GitHub reactions (👀 = in-progress, 👍 = approved) and unresolved review threads filtered by `codex` author login.
Leptos `For` keyed lists require key changes to re-invoke custom render closures — same key with changed row content does not trigger re-render without an explicit revision signal.
Tailwind styled Tabs in both Leptos and Dioxus component crates had indicator CSS bugs where size/translate utilities were not driven by adapter variables.

The workspace uses `cargo xfmt` for formatting and `cargo test -p <crate> --features ssr --test <name>` for SSR tests, plus `WASM_BINDGEN_TEST_ONLY_WEB=1 cargo test --target wasm32-unknown-unknown` for browser wasm tests.
The E2E `wait_for_tab_absent` helper was masking WebDriver failures by treating all errors as absence — only timeout should be treated as absence.

code.finding: Row keying in List/Panels did not include a row render revision, so custom renderer closures were not re-invoked on same-key row updates. Fix adds a per-row revision field.
Tailwind tab indicator template had regression where size/translate utilities were not consuming adapter CSS variables.
Same Tailwind indicator regression as Leptos counterpart.
wait_for_tab_absent treated all WebDriver errors (including real failures) as absence; should only treat timeout as absence.
Leptos view! macro does not accept lifetime-containing types directly in component turbofish — requires a local type alias workaround.

workflow: Running multiple `cargo` test jobs in parallel causes build lock contention; sequential execution is required for this repo.
When using the Codex review loop skill, do not hand off mid-run — stay in the polling loop until a terminal state (👍 or actionable threads) is reached.
Replying to PR review threads requires the comment `databaseId` (for `in_reply_to`) and the GraphQL thread `id` (for `resolveReviewThread`) — these are different identifiers fetched separately.
The skill protocol requires resolving all previous Codex threads (unresolved count = 0) before posting a fresh `@codex review` trigger.
Regression tests should be written first (red) before implementing fixes to pin the failure, then implementation makes them green.

friction: Initial session stopped polling too early while Codex 👀 review was still active — required correction and restart of the polling loop.
Parallel `cargo test` invocations caused Cargo shared build lock contention, forcing sequential test runs.
Leptos `view!` macro turbofish syntax rejects lifetime-containing types directly, requiring a local type alias workaround that was discovered only at compile time.
Codex review ran unusually long (multiple 2-minute poll cycles, >15 minutes) before posting findings, which made it ambiguous whether the review was stalled or in-flight.
GraphQL thread IDs and REST comment databaseIds are different identifiers, requiring a combined query to get both before replying and resolving.
open_items: Codex re-review (@codex review) was triggered but the session ended while still polling — outcome of the second Codex pass is unknown.
The user requested increasing poll cadence to 5 minutes and timeout to 1 hour; polling was in progress but not confirmed complete.
No E2E integration tests were run for the E2E helper change — only a `cargo check` compile verification was done.
The root `group` leakage fix in Tabs was mentioned as a finding but the specific implementation details were not shown in the transcript excerpt.
